statement.en: Digitally aided documentation and analysis of historic Iraqi architecture: 20th-century transformations of the Abbasid Palace and the Mustansiriya Madrasa in Baghdad
statement.summary: This research investigates the 20th-century restoration process of two significant historical buildings: the Abbasid Palace and the Mustansiriya Madrasa in Baghdad, Iraq. Both buildings are examples of the late Abbasid architecture, dating back to the 12th and 13th centuries, and were extensively reconstructed during the 20th century to restore their former grandeur. The aim of this study is to explore how the restoration of these sites reflects Iraq’s approach to heritage preservation. Existing literature suggests a relationship between the two sites, and this research aims to test this proposition. To document and analyse these two historic Iraqi buildings, a combination of historical and digital methods is used. Historical research includes archival materials and secondary literature on

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a 2026-dated architectural heritage documentation study combining historical research with digital methods. The axiom itself (relationship between restoration approaches at two sites) is empirically testable but remains under active investigation. The abstract is incomplete, preventing full evaluation of methodological claims. The work is preprint-stage, data-dependent, and subject to archival validation.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 時間を通じて過去と現在のデジタル化が交差するとき、記憶は新しい形で再構築される。
